expectations.text: Pipeline generation: primary KPI is 400 net-new MQLs with an implied CPL target at or below $187.50 ($75K/400) -- the plan benchmarks against the historical $180 CPL from the benchmark report campaign and explains how channel allocation will hit or beat that efficiency
expectations.evidence: The overview sets the primary KPI at 400 net-new MQLs and a target CPL of $187.50, explicitly benchmarked to the historical $180 CPL from the prior report campaign.

expectations.text: Budget efficiency: channel mix allocates $75,000 with per-channel line items, excludes paid search and events as instructed, and weights spend toward the research-driven content format that historically produced 280 MQLs -- not toward blog content which was explicitly called out as ineffective
expectations.evidence: The channel strategy allocates the full $75,000, excludes paid search and events, and repeatedly states that original research is the proven engine while blog content is only derivative support.

expectations.text: Content leverage: lead magnet is anchored in original research (benchmark report, state-of-industry survey, or similar data-driven asset) that builds on the proven benchmark report format, with a clear plan to derive webinar topics, LinkedIn content, email nurture sequences, and syndication assets from that single research pillar
expectations.evidence: The anchor asset is an original research report, and the plan spins out webinars, LinkedIn promotion, email nurture, blogs, and syndication from that single research pillar.

expectations.text: Alignment: sales-marketing integration defines a specific MQL scoring threshold that triggers Salesforce routing to the six mid-market AEs, specifies the Outreach sequence for MQL follow-up, and sets a measurable speed-to-lead SLA -- connecting marketing's 400-MQL target to AE pipeline activity
expectations.evidence: The output defines an MQL threshold, Salesforce routing to six AEs, an Outreach sequence, and a one-business-hour speed-to-lead SLA with escalation rules.

expectations.text: Execution velocity: content calendar fits within the capacity of two content marketers and a half-time designer without overcommitting -- production milestones are sequenced so the research asset is complete before derivative content begins, and no week exceeds feasible output
expectations.evidence: The week-by-week production plan stays within the team's stated capacity and explicitly notes that the research asset is complete before derivative content begins.

expectations.text: Budget efficiency: risk and contingency section includes a time-bound decision gate with a specific MQL pace threshold (e.g., below 100 MQLs by Week 4), a named metric to evaluate, and a prescribed budget reallocation from underperforming to outperforming channels -- preventing quarter-end scramble on a $75K spend
expectations.evidence: The Week 4 review gate uses a specific threshold (below 100 MQLs), names evaluation metrics, and prescribes concrete reallocation actions by channel.
